title:
Here is a 7-day plan for you:
...
content:
Here is a 7-day plan for you:

Day 1: Start with 5-10 minutes of gentle cardio, such as walking or cycling. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 2: Move on to 20-30 minutes of strength training, such as bench presses or squats. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 3: Spend 15-30 minutes on rest days. 

Day 4: Do 30-60 minutes of light cardio, such as brisk walking or cycling. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 5: Do 10-20 minutes of strength training, such as dumbbell exercises or resistance bands. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 6: Do 20-40 minutes of light cardio, such as brisk walking or cycling. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 7: Do 40-60 minutes of strength training, such as resistance band exercises or bodyweight exercises. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 8: Do 10-20 minutes of light cardio, such as brisk walking or cycling. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 9: Do 20-40 minutes of strength training, such as dumbbell exercises or resistance bands. You can do this every morning before work.

Day 10: Do 30-60 minutes of strength training, such as resistance band exercises or bodyweight exercises. You can do this every morning before work.

Remember, exercise is important, but it's also important to prioritize heart health by avoiding the symptoms of a heart attack. If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to ask!
